preamble: The transformation of a voiced aspirated bilabial plosive (bʱ) into a voiceless glottal fricative (h) is a notable instance of extreme lenition. This sound change involves the complete loss of the original consonant's voicing, aspiration, and bilabial articulation, resulting in a minimal glottal fricative. It represents a significant reduction in articulatory effort and phonetic complexity.
phoneticEffects: Lenition, devoicing, deaspiration, glottalization, place assimilation (bilabial to glottal)

examples.note: A preferred weakening of intervocalic or interior voiced aspirated stops, including /bʱ/, to /h/ was observed in certain western dialects of Old Indo-Aryan.
